steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Quantitative) question. The researchers concluded that oxygenation may have been important in the Alboran Sea decline of L. pertusa but not in the Mauritanian coast decline. The blank must be completed with graph data that justifies this asymmetric conclusion. Key fact from the passage: the Mn/Ca ratio inversely correlates with oxygenation — rising Mn/Ca means falling oxygen.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — a substantial increase in oxygenation in the Alboran Sea corresponded with the local decline in L. pertusa, but the opposite relationship between oxygenation and L. pertusa was found near the Mauritanian coast.: The graph shows Mn/Ca rising sharply in the Alboran Sea around the time of the coral's decline — meaning oxygenation decreased, not increased. This directly contradicts the graph. ✗
  • Choice B — L. pertusa declined in the Alboran Sea during a period of substantial local decline in oxygenation, but L. pertusa declined near the Mauritanian coast during a period of little local change in oxygenation.: The graph shows Mn/Ca rising substantially in the Alboran Sea around 9,000 years ago (oxygenation fell), while Mn/Ca in Mauritanian samples remained nearly flat around 11,000 years ago (oxygenation stable). This asymmetry directly supports the researchers' conclusion. ✓
  • Choice C — oxygenation in the Alboran Sea was higher before the decline in L. pertusa than after the decline, whereas oxygenation near the Mauritanian coast was relatively low both before and after the decline in L. pertusa.: While the first clause is consistent with the Alboran graph, the second is not: Mauritanian Mn/Ca was consistently low (meaning oxygenation was high, not low) throughout the period studied. ✗
  • Choice D — oxygenation in the Alboran Sea tended to be substantially higher than oxygenation near the Mauritanian coast during the period studied.: The graph shows Alboran Mn/Ca was higher than Mauritanian Mn/Ca, which means Alboran oxygenation was lower — the opposite of this claim. Also, a comparison between locations is irrelevant to the conclusion about within-location changes. ✗
B is correct.
